steps.title: Pin down the claim
steps.content: The passage states Camilla experiences a change in how she perceives her immediate surroundings. The correct quotation must show (1) the same surroundings as before and (2) a new, different perception of them.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — “Camilla’s tongue clove to her mouth, heavy and damp as a rag; she could not utter a word.”: Describes a physical sensation (paralysis of speech), not a changed perception of surroundings. ✗
  • Choice B — “All nature was as she had found it before, evening upon evening; but in the stones and the dry weeds, amid the fragrance of the air and the light whir of falling leaves, Camilla sensed a new strangeness, a vast desolation in everything about her.”: "All nature was as she had found it before" confirms the surroundings are unchanged; "sensed a new strangeness, a vast desolation" shows her perception of them has shifted. Both required elements are present. ✓
  • Choice C — “A dead leaf shook slowly loose from the crest of a tree swinging slowly on the wind, fell like a small dead butterfly at her feet. [Camilla] bent down and took it in her fingers.”: Depicts a physical action (picking up a leaf) without any stated contrast between a former and current perception of surroundings. ✗
  • Choice D — “[Camilla] closed her eyes fast to hold back the tears that welled up in them. Then, with the back of her hand, she wiped her wet cheeks, and just as she had done three days ago, fled with all the swiftness of a young deer.”: Shows an emotional reaction and physical flight, not a changed perception of her surroundings. ✗
That matches Choice B — “All nature was as she had found it before, evening upon evening; but in the stones and the dry weeds, amid the fragrance of the air and the light whir of falling leaves, Camilla sensed a new strangeness, a vast desolation in everything about her.”.
